perturbation.original_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs (British Thermal Units) at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs on a Lower Heating Value basis.
perturbation.changed_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by a single Bloom System, measured in BTUs (British Thermal Units) at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs on a Lower Heating Value basis.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Efficiency" originally considered the electricity produced by the entire Portfolio. Modifying the definition to focus on "a single Bloom System" creates a contradiction because subsequent sections, like Section 2.7 (Efficiency Warranty) and Exhibit E (Efficiency Warranty Claim Example Calculation), rely on the original definition related to the portfolio. Now, if a single Bloom System's efficiency calculation is used, the Efficiency Warranty for the entire Portfolio becomes ambiguous and difficult to enforce. This change introduces a discrepancy between how efficiency is calculated for warranty purposes and how it is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Warranty Period, Operator shall determine for each full calendar month (the "Efficiency Warranty Period") within five (5) Business Days after the end of such month whether the Portfolio has performed at the Minimum Efficiency Level (the "Efficiency Warranty"); provided that the Efficiency Bank shall be utilized to the extent necessary to meet the Efficiency Warranty. If the Minimum Efficiency Level has not been met during such Efficiency Warranty Period, then Operator shall so notify Owner in writing of the basis of its determination and Owner may make a claim under Section 2.5. An example of an Efficiency Warranty calculation for purposes of a Section 2.5 claim is attached as Appendix E.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined efficiency based on the entire Portfolio, while the changed text defines it based on a single Bloom System. Section 2.7 explicitly states that 'Operator shall determine for each full calendar month... whether the Portfolio has performed at the Minimum Efficiency Level'. By changing the definition of 'Efficiency' to refer to a single Bloom System, the changed text directly contradicts Section 2.7, which relies on the efficiency of the entire Portfolio. This introduces a significant inconsistency in how efficiency is calculated and makes it impossible to enforce the efficiency warranty based on the original, portfolio-level definition.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") (a) shall commence on the first day of the Warranty Period for the first Bloom System to achieve Commencement of Operation and (b) shall, unless terminated earlier under Section 4.1 of this Agreement or unless extended by mutual agreement of the Parties, terminate on the date that is the last day of the Warranty Period for the last Bloom System to achieve Commencement of Operation.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement (the "Term") (a) shall commence on the first day of the Warranty Period for the first Bloom System to achieve Commencement of Operation and (b) shall, unless terminated earlier under Section 4.1 of this Agreement or unless extended by mutual agreement of the Parties, terminate five years after the start date, regardless of any warranty periods or commencement of operation end dates for Bloom Systems.
perturbation.explanation: The original text ties the termination of the agreement to the Warranty Period of the last Bloom System to achieve Commencement of Operation, ensuring the agreement covers all systems until their warranties expire. Changing the termination to a fixed five-year period creates a contradiction. This creates uncertainty because Bloom Systems warranties could extend beyond the five-year Term, leading to disputes regarding maintenance and performance obligations after the Term expires.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement or the MESPA, each of the Facility Service Warranty, the Efficiency Warranty, and the Power Performance Warranty shall terminate with respect to a Bloom System immediately upon termination of the Warranty Period for such Bloom System; provided that any claims under this Agreement that accrued before such termination shall survive such termination until the resolution of those claims. Operator shall be under no obligation for any Facility Service Warranty, Efficiency Warranty or Power Performance Warranty for a Bloom System during any period for which such Bloom System's Service Fees have not been paid in full.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement's termination clause from being tied to the warranty period of the last Bloom System to a fixed five-year period. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which states that warranties terminate with respect to a Bloom System immediately upon the termination of the Warranty Period for such Bloom System. The changed text creates a situation where warranties could extend beyond the five-year term, creating uncertainty about obligations and enforceability after the Term expires, directly impacting the terms of the warranties as defined in the 'Contradicted Text.'

perturbation.original_text: Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"). With respect to each year of such Facility's Warranty Period, the Service Fees shall be invoiced not later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the calendar month in which the anniversary of the date of Commencement of Operations for such Bloom System occurred, and shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice. Interest shall accrue daily on the Service Fees not paid when due, at the lesser of the monthly rate of one and five-tenths percent (1.5%) or the highest rate permissible by law on such unpaid balance. Operator shall be under no obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for the then-current warranty year.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"). With respect to the five years after the start date, the Service Fees shall be invoiced not later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the calendar month in which the anniversary of the start date occurred, and shall be payable within one year of invoice. Interest shall not accrue daily on the Service Fees not paid when due. Operator shall be under obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for any year.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the service fees needs to be invoiced and paid on an annual basis. Changing the 'Warranty Period' to 'five years after the start date', creates a contradiction, since it does not refer to the actual end of the warranty period of each system, but to an arbitrary fixed 5 year date. The service fees should be paid within thirty (30) days of invoice. It also states "Operator shall be under no obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for the then-current warranty year." Changing it to "Operator shall be under obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for any year.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"). With respect to each year of such Facility's Warranty Period, the Service Fees shall be invoiced not later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the calendar month in which the anniversary of the date of Commencement of Operations for such Bloom System occurred, and shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice. Interest shall accrue daily on the Service Fees not paid when due, at the lesser of the monthly rate of one and five-tenths percent (1.5%) or the highest rate permissible by law on such unpaid balance. Operator shall be under no obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for the then-current warranty year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces several contradictions. First, it changes 'Warranty Period' to a fixed 'five years after the start date', contradicting the original text and creating uncertainty about when service fees should be paid for systems with warranty periods longer or shorter than five years. Second, it changes the payment timeline from 'within thirty (30) days of invoice' to 'within one year of invoice', creating a direct contradiction regarding payment deadlines. Third, it reverses the obligation for the operator to provide services, stating they 'shall be under obligation' even if fees are unpaid, directly contradicting the original text which allowed them to withhold services. These changes create inconsistencies in payment timing, the duration of the service fee schedule, and the operator's obligation to provide services.
